one_liner: LULU -2.8% to $114 underperforms on a strong market day — Wyckoff flags Accumulation Phase B; DCF at $126 shows modest upside but EMA50/200 are both overhead resistance.

price_action.intraday_story: LULU opened near the high and sold down steadily to close at $114.29, near the session low. Down 2.79% on a day the S&P was up 1.64% — significant relative weakness suggesting ongoing distribution or sector rotation out of consumer discretionary.

news_catalyst.headline: No Significant News — Consumer Discretionary Underperformance Continues
news_catalyst.summary: No material LULU-specific news. The stock is declining on relative weakness vs. the tech-led rally. Consumer discretionary names like LULU have been underperforming as higher memory costs (from Micron's pricing cycle) and inflation concerns weigh on premium apparel demand expectations.

technical.summary: Wyckoff Phase B with below-average volume on declining price is an interesting setup — volume drying up during a price decline suggests sellers are exhausted rather than aggressive. The lower BB at $104.31 is the key downside level. RSI at 42.3 is below-neutral but not oversold. DCF at $126 shows 9.6% upside — modest.

wyckoff_narrative: Accumulation Phase B with low-volume price decline is the Wyckoff 'secondary test' pattern — price tests prior lows but volume is insufficient to break through, suggesting the supply overhang is being absorbed. The $104–$112 zone is the accumulation range. A close below $104 on elevated volume would invalidate the accumulation thesis.

valuation.summary: DCF at $126 — 9.6% upside — is not compelling enough to enter against an intermediate downtrend. Wait for EMA50 ($123.43) reclaim before considering accumulation.

key_strengths: Premium brand with loyal customer base and strong international expansion
B grade reflects solid profitability and manageable debt
key_concerns: Relative market weakness is a red flag on a broadly positive day
Consumer discretionary headwinds from sticky inflation
fundamentals.summary: Grade B fundamentals but technical picture doesn't support adding here. Watch for EMA50 reclaim or a proper Wyckoff Spring (final shakeout low) before acting.

risks: Relative weakness on positive market day suggests ongoing institutional distribution
EMA50 at $123.43 is 8% overhead — recovery requires sustained buying pressure
Consumer spending slowdown would directly impact premium athleisure demand
